items.title: Mutable defaults
items.avoid: Bare mutable defaults in dataclass fields.
items.example: `field(default_factory=list)` ensures each `Job()` gets its own empty list — not the same shared one across every instance.

items.title: Picking a container
items.avoid: Forcing every job into a plain dict/list.
items.prefer: Counter for tallies, defaultdict for grouping, deque for queues/windows.
items.example: Top tokens by frequency? `Counter(tokens).most_common(10)` — one line, O(n log k), and the intent is unmistakable.

items.title: Immutable config
items.why: Safe to share and hashable for caching/keys.
items.example:
```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Settings: model: str = 'gpt-4o'; temp: float = 0.7
variant = replace(base, temp=1.2)  # new value; base untouched; both hashable
```

items.title: Existence checks
items.avoid: Indexing a defaultdict to test membership.
items.why: Indexing inserts the key as a side effect.
items.example: `if k in d:` is read-only. `d[k]` on a `defaultdict(list)` *creates* `d[k] = []` even if you 'just' looked. Use the right check for the intent.
